title: NAC Quarantine and Remediation Duration
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="nac:quarantine"
| eval duration_min=(released_time - quarantine_time)/60
| stats avg(duration_min) as avg_mins, count by posture_violation, remediation_result
| where avg_mins > 60
| table posture_violation, remediation_result, count, avg_mins
description: Long quarantine or remediation times affect user productivity. Monitoring duration supports process improvement and exception handling.
value: IT operations teams measure exactly how long devices spend in quarantine broken down by failure type, identifying which posture failures take longest to remediate and whether remediation infrastructure (patch servers, AV update servers) is performing within SLA targets.
implementation: Ingest NAC quarantine and release events. Compute time in quarantine and remediation success. Alert when average duration exceeds threshold. Report on violation types and remediation rate.
visualization: Table (quarantine duration by violation), Bar chart (avg duration), Pie chart (remediation outcome).
knownFalsePositives: Posture failures during scheduled patch cycles, OS upgrades, or after AV definition rotations.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Cisco ISE quarantine and posture events in `index=nac` with `sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog`. Key fields: `CallingStationID`, `SelectedAuthorizationProfiles`, `PostureStatus`, `PostureAssessmentFailure`, `UserName`, `OperatingSystem`.
• This UC measures the time dimension: how long devices spend in quarantine, what the median and P95 remediation times are, and which posture failures take longest to fix. This directly impacts user productivity — every minute in quarantine is a minute the user cannot work.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify transition events:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-30d
| where match(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles, "(?i)quarantine")
| stats count
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — Quarantine duration analysis:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-30d
| sort CallingStationID _time
| streamstats current=f last(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles) as prev_profile last(_time) as prev_time last(PostureAssessmentFailure) as quarantine_reason by CallingStationID
| where match(prev_profile, "(?i)quarantine") AND NOT match(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles, "(?i)quarantine")
| eval duration_min=round((_time - prev_time)/60, 0)
| stats avg(duration_min) as avg_min median(duration_min) as median_min perc95(duration_min) as p95_min count as remediations by quarantine_reason
| sort - p95_min
```

Understanding this SPL: Calculates quarantine-to-release duration per failure type. The P95 metric is more actionable than the average because it reveals the worst-case user experience. Typical benchmarks: AV definition update should remediate in <15 minutes (auto-download). OS patches may take 30-60 minutes. Disk encryption enablement can take hours. If any remediation consistently exceeds its expected time, the remediation infrastructure needs attention.

SLA compliance:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" earliest=-30d
| sort CallingStationID _time
| streamstats current=f last(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles) as prev_profile last(_time) as prev_time by CallingStationID
| where match(prev_profile, "(?i)quarantine") AND NOT match(SelectedAuthorizationProfiles, "(?i)quarantine")
| eval duration_min=round((_time - prev_time)/60, 0)
| eval sla_met=if(duration_min <= 60, "Within SLA", "Exceeded SLA")
| stats count by sla_met
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Quarantine a test device, note the time, remediate, note the time. Compare with Splunk.
(b) The duration should match within 5 minutes (ISE log delay).

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Median/P95 remediation time. Row 2 — Duration by failure type. Row 3 — SLA compliance %.
Alert: When P95 remediation time exceeds 120 minutes.

Runbook:
1. **P95 high for AV updates** — Remediation VLAN may not have access to AV update server. Check ACLs.
2. **P95 high for patches** — WSUS server may be slow or unreachable from quarantine VLAN.
3. **Duration increasing over time** — More complex remediation requirements being added without improving infrastructure.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Duration shows 0 minutes** — Instant transitions may mean CoA is triggering before actual remediation. Check ISE posture timer settings.
• **Negative duration** — Time sync issue between ISE nodes. Enable NTP sync.
grandmaExplanation: We time how long it takes for a device to get out of the penalty box. If fixing antivirus takes 5 minutes but fixing missing patches takes 2 hours, we know to invest in making the patch process faster, because every minute in the penalty box is a minute someone cannot work.
